summary: Finished matching the red Koopa shell item translation unit by deleting asm/melee/it/items/itzrshell.s, moving itzrshell.c to Matching in configure.py, and switching obj_files.mk to build the C object. The PR added the remaining C-side item state table it_803F87F0 and spawn/helper function it_802E0488, tightened prototypes and headers for item creation and shell dependencies, and added an item-var union member for shell-specific data at xDD4_itemVar.shell.xE10. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the slice, so intent beyond matching completion is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Deleted the 298-line assembly file asm/melee/it/items/itzrshell.s, including wrapper functions it_802E02E8 through it_802E0468, item state table data it_803F87F0, zero vector rodata it_803B86F8, and the integer-to-float conversion constant it_804DD6C0.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itzrshell.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itzrshell.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itzrshell.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/items/itzrshell.s.o.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F87F0[] in itzrshell.c as a typed C initializer mirroring the deleted assembly data table of state IDs and callbacks from itzgshell functions.

Added Item_GObj* it_802E0488(s32 arg0, s32 arg1, s32 arg2), which creates It_Kind_ZRShell via it_8027B5B0, initializes facing direction, writes shell.xE10, starts animation state 0xC, animates the JObj, and selects behavior based on it_802DDA84 and it_80277040.
Added includes in itzrshell.c for itnokonoko.h, it_266F.h, it_2725.h, it/types.h, and baselib/gobj.h to support the translated function and typed table.
Updated it_8027B5B0 prototype in it_266F.h and it_266F.dox from void* it_8027B5B0(s32, s32, HSD_JObj*, s32, s32) to Item_GObj* it_8027B5B0(s32, s32, HSD_JObj*, Vec3*, s32).
Updated ground.c call to it_8027B5B0 to pass NULL instead of integer 0 for the new Vec3* parameter.
Added itShellVars with padding to offset 0xE10 and int xE10 in itCommonItems.h, and added itShellVars shell to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in it/types.h.
Added declarations for it_80277040 and it_8027C56C in it_2725.h.
Created real contents for itnokonoko.h with an include guard, platform/item includes, and bool it_802DDA84(Item_GObj*) declaration.
Expanded itzgshell.h declarations to include many state callback functions used by it_803F87F0, with bool return types where used as state callbacks.
Updated itzrshell.h to include it/item.h, add declarations for it_802E0448, it_802E0488, and extern ItemStateTable it_803F87F0[], and remove argument names from prototypes.
smart_moves: The deleted asm showed many one-call trampoline functions; the C file keeps those wrappers simple, allowing the translation to focus on the only substantial remaining function it_802E0488 and the state table.
Representing the deleted raw .4byte state table as ItemStateTable it_803F87F0[] preserves type information and makes callback return types explicit in headers.

Changing it_8027B5B0 from void* plus integer position argument to Item_GObj* plus Vec3* made call sites and the translated spawn code type-correct; the ground.c NULL update is a small but important fallout fix.
Adding itShellVars at the xDD4 item-var union gave the xE10 store a named layout path, it->xDD4_itemVar.shell.xE10, instead of keeping a raw offset.
The local Vec3 pos = { 0, 0, 0 } replaced the deleted rodata zero vector data and matches the item creation call pattern in C.
Using PAD_STACK(4) inside it_802E0488 is an explicit matching tactic to reproduce the stack layout needed after translating the assembly.
decomp_lessons: When matching an item file, update both build-selection layers: configure.py's Object(Matching, ...) and obj_files.mk's asm-to-src object path.
Raw assembly state tables can often become typed ItemStateTable arrays, but doing so may require broadening sibling headers to declare all callback symbols with correct bool/void return types.
A spawn function that calls item factory code may force global prototype cleanup; here it_8027B5B0 needed an Item_GObj* return and a Vec3* spawn-position parameter.
If a union field access is needed at a high offset, add a narrow item-var struct with padding instead of scattering raw offset assumptions in C.
Integer zero arguments at pointer positions should be updated to NULL after prototype refinement to avoid type mismatch and improve documentation.
Simple assembly wrappers can be matched as direct C calls to the underlying implementation functions, but the exported symbol names must still be declared in the item header.
naming_conventions: Shell-specific item vars were named itShellVars and installed as the xDD4_itemVar.shell union member.
The unknown shell field remained offset-based as xE10, matching existing decomp convention for unidentified struct fields.
Function names remained address-based, such as it_802E0488, it_802DEEF4, and it_8027B5B0, because no semantic names were introduced in the evidence.
Headers used the project include-guard style _itzrshell_h_ and _itnokonoko_h_.
Prototype cleanup removed parameter names in item headers where surrounding declarations use unnamed typed parameters.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the assembly file only after all text and data from itzrshell.s were represented in C and the build switched to the C object.
Translated assembly .4byte callback table into ItemStateTable initializer rows ending with { 4, NULL, NULL, NULL }.
Replaced assembly rodata zero vector it_803B86F8 with a local stack Vec3 pos = { 0, 0, 0 } passed by address.
Used PAD_STACK(4) in it_802E0488 to preserve stack/codegen behavior for matching.

Matched the assembly's signed integer to float behavior for facing direction through it->facing_dir = arg2 followed by it_8027C56C(it_gp, it->facing_dir), rather than exposing the deleted sdata2 constant directly.

Preserved branch structure in it_802E0488: after spawn, initialize animation and JObj, then use it_802DDA84 to choose between it_802762B0/it_802762BC paths and it_80277040 to choose it_802DE0F0 versus it_802DEEF4.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR merged quickly, but the slice does not include reviewer rationale or requested changes.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename itShellVars or identify xE10 semantically.
Search for other item factory prototype cleanups around it_8027B5B0 and Vec3* spawn positions.
Search for similar shell item matching work in itzgshell.c and itnokonoko.c to confirm shared state table callback conventions.
Check whether other ground or stage code still passes integer 0 where NULL is expected after typed prototype updates.
